title: Biography – Nigel Crisp
text_excerpt: Biography – Nigel Crisp Skip to content Nigel Crisp Lord Crisp KCB Menu Home Biography Global Health Health and Health Systems Global Health in Parliament Nursing Now Publications Contact @lordnigelcrisp Biography Lord Nigel Crisp is an independent crossbench member of the House of Lords where he co-chairs the All-Party Parliamentary Group on Global Health. He also co-chairs Nursing Now , the global campaign on nursing. He was previously Chief Executive of the English NHS and Permanent Secretary of the UK Department of Health – the largest health organisation in the world with 1.3 million employees – where he led major reforms between 2000 and 2006. Lord Crisp is a Senior Fellow at the Institute for Healthcare Improvement, an Honorary Professor at the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine and a Foreign Associate of the US National Academy of Medicine. He was formerly a Distinguished Visiting Fellow at the Harvard School of Public Health and Regent’s Lecturer at Berkeley. His publications on global health include Turning the world upside down – the search for global health in the 21st Century ; Global Health Partnerships ; One World Health – an overview of global health after Global Health Partnerships ; and, edited with Francis Omaswa, African Health Leaders – making change and claiming the future . He described his time as Chief Executive of the NHS in 24 Hours to Save the NHS – the Chief Executive’s account of reform 2000 – 2006 . A Cambridge philosophy graduate, he worked in community development and industry before joining the NHS in 1986. He has worked in mental health as well as acute services and from 1993 to 1997 was Chief Executive of the Oxford Radcliffe Hospital NHS Trust, one of the UK’s leading academic medical centres. Affiliations Current affiliations include: Co-chair – All-Party Parliamentary Group on Global Health Co-chair – Nursing Now Chair of the Advisory Board of the Zambia UK Health Workforce Alliance Member of Advisory Board of the African Centre for Health and Social Transformation Member of Advisory Board of Uganda UK Health Alliance Honorary Professor at the London School for Hygiene and Tropical Medicine Senior Fellow of the institute for Healthcare Improvement Honorary Fellowships at St John’s College, Cambridge University; Royal College of Physicians; Royal College of Pathologists; Royal College of Obstetricians and Gynaecologists; Royal College of Psychiatrists; the Faculty of Public Health; and the Institute of Healthcare Management Previous affiliations include: Chair of Sightsavers International 2007-2015 Distinguished Visiting Fellow at the Harvard School of Public Health Regents Lecturer at the University of California at Berkeley Latest News Turning the World Upside Down Again – global health in a time of pandemics, climate change and political turmoil will be published in late March 2022. It follows on to Turning the World Upside Down published in 2010. The Nursing Now Challeng e was launched in June 2021 as a follow on from the Nightingale Challenge (part of the legacy of Nursing Now) and aims to provide leadership development opportunities for more than 100,000 nurses and midwives globally.
